1	---
2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am mandatory in the review phase. Dispatch one checker per .lean
11	    file that received prover work this iteration — every file, no
12	    exceptions. Skipping any prover-touched file is a review-phase
13	    failure, not editorial discretion. Each dispatch is independent
14	    and parallel-able.
15	  - Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog
16	    flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this
17	    iter.
18	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
19	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
20	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
21	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
22	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide
23	    formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly
24	    needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,
25	    the plan agent should dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing
26	    subagent to address it.
27	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on
28	    the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the
29	    plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.
30	---
31	
32	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
33	
34	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
35	
36	## Your Job
37	
38	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
39	
40	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
41	
42	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
43	
44	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
45	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
46	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
47	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
48	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
49	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
50	
51	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
52	
53	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
54	
55	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
56	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
57	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
58	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
59	
60	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
61	
62	## Directive Format
63	
64	```markdown
65	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
66	
67	## Slug
68	<slug>
69	
70	## Lean file
71	<path/to/file.lean>
72	
73	## Blueprint chapter
74	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
75	
76	## Known issues
77	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
78	```
79	
80	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
81	
82	## What you do
83	
84	1. Read your directive.
85	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
86	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
87	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
88	   - It exists.
89	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
90	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
91	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
92	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
93	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
94	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
95	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
96	   - `axiom` declarations.
97	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
98	
99	You may use:
100	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
101	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
102	
103	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
104	
105	## Report format
106	
107	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
108	
109	```markdown
110	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
111	
112	## Slug
113	<slug>
114	
115	## Iteration
116	<NNN>
117	
118	## Files audited
119	- Lean: <path>
120	- Blueprint: <path>
121	
122	## Per-declaration
123	
124	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
125	
126	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
127	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
128	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
129	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
130	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
131	
132	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
133	
134	## Red flags
135	
136	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
137	
138	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
139	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
140	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
141	- ...
142	
143	### Excuse-comments
144	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
145	- ...
146	
147	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
148	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
149	
150	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
151	
152	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
153	
154	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
155	
156	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
157	
158	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
159	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
160	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
161	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
162	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
167	
168	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
169	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
170	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
171	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
172	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
173	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
174	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
175	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
176	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
177	
178	Overall verdict: one sentence.
179	```
180	
181	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and the blueprint-review subagent's `dispatcher_notes` in the catalog) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
182	
183	## Return value
184	
185	Your final assistant message:
186	
187	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
188	- The path to your full report.
189	
190	## Reminders
191	
192	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to the catalog's code-audit and blueprint-review subagents.
193	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
194	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
195	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.
196

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials
7	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
8	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Proper
11	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Irreducible
12	import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Functor.ReflectsIso.Balanced
13	
14	/-!
15	# Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme
16	
17	The cotangent space at the identity of a smooth proper geometrically
18	irreducible group scheme `G` over a base field `k`, defined as a
19	`k`-vector space. Mathematically, this is the pullback of the relative
20	cotangent sheaf `Ω_{G/k}` along the identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`;
21	the iter-130 Lean realisation (Replacement (B), per
22	`analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`) computes it as the base change to
23	`k` of the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` on a
24	smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image.
25	
26	This is piece (i.a) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (see
27	`blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i): sub-lemma
28	decomposition for iter-128+ build" and STRATEGY.md § M2.body-pile).
29	
30	This file contains the definition `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` below, the
31	structural-shape acceptance lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`
32	below, and the companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`
33	below (rank = relative dimension `n` from
34	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, closed iter-132).
35	
36	## Status (iter-132 close: rank lemma)
37	
38	`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is fully constructed
39	(no `sorry`). The iter-128 body (presheaf evaluated at the top open then
40	extended scalars to `k`) was found in iter-129 to compute the zero
41	`k`-module for every `G` in the target class with `n ≥ 1` (`Spec` of a
42	field is a single point, so the pulled-back presheaf collapses to `k`
43	and the Kähler module of the identity `k → k` is zero). The iter-130
44	fix-up replaced it with **Replacement (B): affine-chart base change** —
45	extract an affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around the identity-section image
46	on which the algebraic Kähler module is free of rank `n` (via
47	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`), then base-change to `k` along the
48	identity section restricted to `V`. The iter-130 realisation passed
49	through `Classical.choice` to bridge `Prop` and `Type`, which left the
50	elaborated body with `Classical.choice` as its outermost head symbol —
51	opaque to downstream `unfold`/`whnf`. The **iter-131 fix-up** refactors
52	the body to a pure-term `noncomputable def`: `let`-bindings of
53	`Classical.choose`/`.choose_spec` on the `smooth_locally_free_omega`
54	existential expose `U, V, e, ψV` as named-but-opaque accessors, while
55	the outer expression is the explicit
56	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`. The
57	signature, set in iter-129 (free `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`
58	binder), is preserved. The iter-131 body shape is witnessed by
59	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below.
60	
61	The **iter-132 prover lane** closed the companion rank lemma
62	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below) against the
63	iter-131 body via a parallel `Classical.choose`-chain extraction on
64	`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, reducing through
65	`Module.finrank_baseChange` + `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq` to pin the
66	`k`-finrank of the cotangent space at the identity to the relative
67	dimension `n` from the `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
68	
69	## References
70	
71	- Blueprint chapter: `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` § "Piece (i)".
72	- Analogist source-of-truth: `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
73	  (Replacement (B) construction; iter-130 prover lane closure chain).
74	- Project infrastructure consumed:
75	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`
76	  (in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`).
77	- Mathlib `GrpObj` API: `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_`,
78	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth`.
79	- Mathlib Kähler / standard-smooth pieces (consumed via
80	  `smooth_locally_free_omega`):
81	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`,
82	  `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential`.
83	-/
84	
85	set_option autoImplicit false
86	
87	universe u
88	
89	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
90	open TopologicalSpace Opposite
91	
92	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj
93	
94	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
95	
96	/-- The **cotangent space at the identity** of a smooth proper geometrically
97	irreducible group scheme `G` over `k`, as a `k`-vector space (returned as
98	`ModuleCat k`).
99	
100	Mathematically: the base change to `k` of the algebraic Kähler module
101	`Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` of a smooth affine chart `V ⊆ G.left` around
102	the identity-section image. By the forward Jacobian criterion
103	(`Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega`, consuming
104	`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`),
105	this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G, V)`, hence its base
106	change is a finitely-generated free `k`-module of rank `n`; the
107	iter-132 companion rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (at
108	line 244 below) pins the rank to `n` from the
109	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]` instance.
110	
111	The bracket / Lie-algebra structure is NOT packaged here — only the
112	underlying `k`-module is needed for the rigidity argument. Downstream
113	consumers that need the dual tangent space `𝔤` may take
114	`Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)`.
115	
116	**Construction (iter-130 prover lane; Replacement (B), affine-chart base
117	change).** The body is built as follows:
118	1. The identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G` of the `GrpObj` structure gives
119	   a scheme morphism `ηleft : Spec k ⟶ G.left` (using the definitional
120	   identification `(𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))).left = Spec (.of k)`).
121	2. Take `x₀ := ηleft default ∈ G.left`, the image of the unique point of
122	   `Spec (.of k)` (which is a singleton because `k` is a field, so
123	   `PrimeSpectrum k` is `Unique`).
124	3. By `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` (forward Jacobian criterion,
125	   project lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.Differentials`), there exists an
126	   affine open `V ⊆ G.left` containing `x₀`, an affine open `U ⊆ Spec k`
127	   with `V ≤ G.hom⁻¹U`, and the `appLE` algebra
128	   `Γ(Spec k, U) → Γ(G, V)` exhibits `Ω[Γ(G, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]` as a
129	   free `Γ(G, V)`-module of rank `n`.
130	4. Restricting `ηleft` to `V` yields a ring map
131	   `ηleft.appLE V ⊤ _ : Γ(G, V) → Γ(Spec k, ⊤)`; composing with the
132	   canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) gives
133	   `ψV : Γ(G, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k`.
134	5. Extension of scalars from `Γ(G, V)` to `k` along `ψV` of the algebraic
135	   Kähler module gives the cotangent space at the identity as a
136	   `k`-module.
137	
138	**Caveat on canonicity.** The chart `V` used in the body is extracted
139	via `Classical.choose` from `smooth_locally_free_omega`'s existential.
140	The resulting `k`-module is therefore non-canonical *as a value*. What
141	the iter-131 body refactor guarantees is that the body's **structural
142	shape** — a base change to `k` of the chart-level Kähler module — is
143	exposed to downstream consumers: after `unfold cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
144	and delta-reduction of the `let`-bindings, the outer head symbol is
145	`(ModuleCat.extendScalars _).obj (ModuleCat.of _ Ω[_ ⁄ _])`. This is
146	formalized as `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` below, which
147	the iter-132 rank lemma `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244
148	below) rewrites against. For the live consumer
149	(rigidity over `k`, via `rigidity_over_kbar`), the existence of a
150	rank-`n` free `k`-module is the only structural content needed, so
151	chart-canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent)
152	presentation via the stalk-side cotangent `𝔪 / 𝔪²` is the content of
153	the blueprint's `lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge` (deferred to iter-130+).
154	
155	This compiles to a `ModuleCat k` with no `sorry`. The rank (`= n`) is
156	now pinned by `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` (line 244 below;
157	closed iter-132). The free/finite structural properties
158	(`Module.Free k`, `Module.Finite k`) are content for follow-up companion
159	lemmas (not yet in this file); the structural-shape accessibility needed
160	by those lemmas is witnessed here by
161	`cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars` (line 198 below). -/
162	noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
163	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom]
164	    [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
165	    ModuleCat k :=
166	  -- Identity section `η_G : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`; on schemes this is `Spec k ⟶ G.left`.
167	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
168	  -- Image in `G.left` of the unique point of `Spec (.of k)` (`Spec` of a field).
169	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
170	  -- `Classical.choose`-chain through the `Prop`-level existential output of
171	  -- `smooth_locally_free_omega`. Each `let` names one component (chart `U`,
172	  -- chart `V`, the appLE inclusion `e`, and `hxV : x₀ ∈ V`) so the outer
173	  -- expression below stays in a pure-term shape — no `Classical.choice`
174	  -- wrapper at the top level (iter-131 body-shape refactor).
175	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
176	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
177	  let h₁ := h.choose_spec
178	  let V : G.left.Opens := h₁.choose
179	  let h₂ := h₁.choose_spec
180	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h₂.choose
181	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h₂.choose_spec.1
182	  -- The pre-image of `V` under `ηleft` is all of `Spec (.of k)`, since
183	  -- `Spec` of a field has a unique point and that point lies in `V`.
184	  let htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
185	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
186	    exact hxV
187	  -- Ring map `ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ k` from the identity section restricted to
188	  -- `V`, composed with the canonical iso `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k`.
189	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
190	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
191	  -- Equip `Γ(G.left, V)` with the `Γ(Spec k, U)`-algebra structure from
192	  -- `appLE` on `G.hom`.
193	  letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
194	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
195	  -- Body: base-change the algebraic Kähler module `Ω[Γ(G.left, V) / Γ(Spec k, U)]`
196	  -- from `Γ(G.left, V)` to `k` along `ψV`. By `smooth_locally_free_omega`
197	  -- (consuming `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`)
198	  -- this Kähler module is free of rank `n` over `Γ(G.left, V)`, hence its
199	  -- base change is free of rank `n` over `k`.
200	  (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj
201	    (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])
202	
203	/-- Defensive lemma: `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` is definitionally equal to its
204	explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module form. The chart `V`, the algebra
205	structure, and the Kähler module are opaque (`Classical.choose`-extracted) but

211	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
212	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
213	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
214	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) (V : G.left.Opens) (e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
215	        (htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤
216	          (CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]) ⁻¹ᵁ V),
217	      letI : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
218	        (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
219	      (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G : ModuleCat k) =
220	        (ModuleCat.extendScalars
221	          ((CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]).appLE V ⊤ htop ≫
222	            (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom).hom).obj
223	          (ModuleCat.of Γ(G.left, V) Ω[Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec (.of k), U)]) := by
224	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
225	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
226	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
227	  let hxV : x₀ ∈ h.choose_spec.choose := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
228	  refine ⟨h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
229	    fun s _ => ?_, rfl⟩
230	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) s ∈ h.choose_spec.choose
231	  rw [show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
232	  exact hxV

234	/-- **Rank lemma for the cotangent space at the identity.**
235	
236	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `G` over `k` of
237	relative dimension `n`, the `k`-vector space `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G` has
238	`finrank` equal to `n`.
239	
240	**Proof strategy (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` §
241	`lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`, Steps 1+2 live closure path).**
242	
243	* **Step 1 (chart-side Kähler rank).** Reproduce the iter-131 body's
244	  `Classical.choose`-chain on `Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega` to extract
245	  the same chart witnesses `U`, `V`, `e` plus `hfree : Module.Free Γ(G, V)
246	  Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` and `hrank : Module.rank ... = n`. Apply
247	  `Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank` to get
248	  `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…] = n`.
249	
250	* **Step 2 (base-change preserves finrank).** The body of
251	  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is by construction the `extendScalars ψ_V`
252	  base change of `Ω[Γ(G, V) ⁄ Γ(Spec k, U)]` from `Γ(G, V)` to `k`. Apply
253	  `Module.finrank_baseChange` to push `Module.finrank Γ(G, V) Ω[…]` to
254	  `Module.finrank k (k ⊗_{Γ(G,V)} Ω[…])`, discharging the `Module.Free`
255	  hypothesis via `hfree` and the `Nontrivial Γ(G, V)` hypothesis from the
256	  existence of the ring map `ψ_V : Γ(G, V) → k` into the field `k`. -/
257	theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
258	    [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
259	    [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] :
260	    Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (n := n) G) = n := by
261	  -- Reproduce the same `Classical.choose`-chain as the body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`.
262	  let ηleft : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left := CategoryTheory.CommaMorphism.left η[G]
263	  let x₀ : G.left := (ConcreteCategory.hom ηleft.base) default
264	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
265	  let U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose
266	  let V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose
267	  let e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose
268	  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
269	  -- Algebra structure on `Γ(G.left, V)` over `Γ(Spec k, U)` (matches the body's `letI`).
270	  letI algGV : Algebra ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U) ↥Γ(G.left, V) :=
271	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE G.hom U V e).hom.toAlgebra
272	  -- Freeness + rank witnesses from the existential.
273	  have hfree : Module.Free ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] :=
274	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
275	  have hrank : Module.rank ↥Γ(G.left, V) Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)] = (n : Cardinal) :=
276	    h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
277	  -- ψV ring map (matches the body's `ψV`).
278	  have htop : (⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V := fun s _ => by
279	    rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, show s = default from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
280	    exact hxV
281	  let ψV : Γ(G.left, V) ⟶ CommRingCat.of k :=
282	    ηleft.appLE V ⊤ htop ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of k)).hom
283	  letI algGVk : Algebra ↥Γ(G.left, V) k := ψV.hom.toAlgebra
284	  -- The Γ(G, V) algebra is Nontrivial because it maps to the field `k`.
285	  haveI : Nontrivial ↥Γ(G.left, V) := ψV.hom.domain_nontrivial
286	  -- The body of `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` is `(extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])`,
287	  -- whose carrier is `TensorProduct Γ(G, V) k Ω[…]` (under `algGVk`). Definitional equality
288	  -- of the carriers reduces the goal to `Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ...) = n`.
289	  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct ↥Γ(G.left, V) k
290	      (Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])) = n
291	  -- Step 2: Base-change preserves finrank.
292	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := ↥Γ(G.left, V))
293	        (M' := Ω[↥Γ(G.left, V) ⁄ ↥Γ(Spec (.of k), U)])]
294	  -- Step 1: Apply rank=n ⇒ finrank=n.
295	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank

311	  `σ = ⟨pr₁, μ⟩ : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G` in `Over (Spec k)` from `GrpObj` data only.

317	  the load-bearing presheaf-of-modules base-change identity
318	  `Ω_{(G ⊗ G)/G} ≅ pr_2^* Ω_{G/k}` on `(G ⊗ G).left`.
319	  NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (~150–300 LOC); chains

327	-/
328	
329	section ShearIso
330	
331	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]
332	
333	/-- **Binary-product shear isomorphism** for a group object `G` in a Cartesian
334	monoidal category: the map
335	`σ = lift (fst G G) μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G ⊗ G`, informally `(a, b) ↦ (a, a · b)`,
336	is an isomorphism. The inverse is
337	`σ⁻¹ = lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)`, informally
338	`(a, b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · b)`.
339	
340	This is the binary-product upgrade of
341	`CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean:277-281`),
342	which packages only the one-input version `(· · f)` for a fixed global element
343	`f : 𝟙_ ⟶ G`. Here both factors of `G ⊗ G` are free.
344	
345	Piece (i.b) Step 1 (per `blueprint/src/chapters/RigidityKbar.tex` Step 1 of
346	`lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises`). NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (no packaged shear
347	iso for binary products in Mathlib; the iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict
348	Decision 1 prescribes this exact construction). -/
349	@[simps]
350	def shearMulRight (G : C) [GrpObj G] : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G where
351	  hom := lift (fst G G) μ
352	  inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)
353	  hom_inv_id := by
354	    -- `(a, b) ↦ (a, a · b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · (a · b)) = (a, b)`.
355	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
356	    · simp
357	    · -- Goal: `(σ.hom ≫ σ.inv) ≫ snd G G = 𝟙 (G ⊗ G) ≫ snd G G`
358	      rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
359	      -- Goal: `σ.hom ≫ (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) = snd G G`
360	      rw [← Category.assoc, comp_lift]
361	      -- Goal: `lift (σ.hom ≫ (fst G G ≫ ι)) (σ.hom ≫ snd G G) ≫ μ = snd G G`
362	      rw [← Category.assoc, lift_fst, lift_snd]
363	      -- Goal: `lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ = snd G G`
364	      -- Trick: use `lift_lift_assoc` with f := fst ≫ ι, g := fst, h := snd, then
365	      -- close by `lift_comp_inv_left` and `lift_comp_one_left`.
366	      rw [show (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
367	          lift (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (fst G G) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from by
368	            rw [MonObj.lift_lift_assoc, lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp],
369	        CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left, MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
370	  inv_hom_id := by
371	    -- `(a, b) ↦ (a, a⁻¹ · b) ↦ (a, a · (a⁻¹ · b)) = (a, b)`.
372	    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
373	    · simp
374	    · -- Goal: `(σ.inv ≫ σ.hom) ≫ snd G G = 𝟙 (G ⊗ G) ≫ snd G G`
375	      rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
376	      -- Goal: `lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ = snd G G`
377	      -- Apply `lift_lift_assoc` (← direction): the RHS form `lift f (lift g h ≫ μ) ≫ μ`
378	      -- rewrites to `lift (lift f g ≫ μ) h ≫ μ` with f := fst, g := fst ≫ ι, h := snd.
379	      rw [show (lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ :
380	          G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
381	          lift (lift (fst G G) (fst G G ≫ ι) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from
382	          (MonObj.lift_lift_assoc (fst G G) (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G)).symm,
383	        CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_right,
384	        MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
385	
386	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
387	lemma shearMulRight_hom_fst (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
388	    (shearMulRight G).hom ≫ fst G G = fst G G := by
389	  simp [shearMulRight]
390	
391	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
392	lemma shearMulRight_hom_snd (G : C) [GrpObj G] :
393	    (shearMulRight G).hom ≫ snd G G = μ := by
394	  simp [shearMulRight]
395	
396	end ShearIso

81	\subsection{Piece (i): sub-lemma decomposition for iter-128+ build}
82	\label{subsec:RigidityKbar_piece_i_decomposition}
83	
84	We decompose piece (i) above into three sub-steps (i.a)\,$\to$\,(i.b)\,$\to$\,(i.c) that are well-suited to staged Lean formalisation. Sub-step (i.a) defines the Lie algebra (as a $k$-vector space) of a group scheme at the identity and pins its rank; sub-step (i.b) globalises that fibre across $G$ via a functorial shear isomorphism; sub-step (i.c) concludes the freeness and rank of the relative cotangent $\Omega_{G/k}$.
85	
86	All three sub-steps are stated over an arbitrary base field $k$ per the iter-127 over-k commitment (see the chapter introduction, \S "Iter-127 over-k commitment"); the prose carefully avoids any reliance on $k$-rational or $\bar k$-rational point lifting, working functorially throughout. Sub-step (i.a) is the natural first target for the iter-128+ prover lane: it has the smallest signature surface (a finite-rank free $k$-module attached to the identity section of $G$), and no scheme-level globalisation.
87	
88	\paragraph{Iter-128 / iter-129 prover lane re-scoping (per \texttt{strategy-critic-iter128} + iter-129 rename).} Sub-step (i.a) was originally a 2-target pair and is now (post-iter-129) a 3-target trio: \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace} (the \emph{definition} of $\mathfrak g^\vee$, plus the finitely-generated-free claim — iter-128 lane, body landed), \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} (the \emph{bridge} to the local-ring cotangent space $\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^{2}$ — iter-130+ build target), and \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} (the \emph{rank-equals-relative-dimension} claim — iter-130+ build target). The iter-128 prover lane attacks ONLY the first: it scaffolds the Lean declaration \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} (renamed iter-129 from the placeholder \texttt{lieAlgebra}; signature relaxed to a free \texttt{\{n : \mathbb N\} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]} binder so downstream consumers of arbitrary relative dimension can instantiate it) and closes its body. The bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} is vestigial on the live path under Replacement (B) (see \S Q4 collapse footer at the rank-lemma proof's end) and remains deferred indefinitely; the rank lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} is the iter-132 prover-lane target (closure path: Step 1 chart-side Kähler rank from \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}'s existential + Step 2 base-change to $k$ via \texttt{Module.finrank\_baseChange}, both detailed in that lemma's proof body). Rationale: (i) bundling definition + bridge + rank into one prover-iter task is borderline-too-ambitious per \texttt{progress-critic-iter128}; (ii) the rank-lemma RHS Lean encoding needs blueprint pinning iter-129 before being prover-ready (it must read \texttt{Module.finrank k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G) = n} parameterised by an explicit \texttt{[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]} instance; the bare $\dim G$ in the prose does not type-check directly because Mathlib has no $\dim G$ function on \texttt{Over (Spec k)}, per \texttt{strategy-critic-iter128} CHALLENGE on (i.a) signature ambiguity).
89	
90	Throughout this subsection, $k$ is an arbitrary field and $G : \Over\,(\Spec k)$ is a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $k$, with identity section $\eta_G : \mathbf{1} \to G$.
91	
92	\begin{lemma}\leanok
93	[(i.a) Cotangent space at the identity of a group scheme]
94	  \label{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}
95	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}
96	  % Lean signature stub (pinned post-iter-129 fixup; relaxed from the
97	  % iter-128 hardcoded rel-dim-1 form so the same declaration serves
98	  % consumers of arbitrary relative dimension, e.g. \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}
99	  % applied to an abelian variety of dimension $g$):
100	  %   noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
101	  %       [CategoryTheory.GrpObj G] {n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]
102	  %       [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom] : ModuleCat k
103	  The pullback along the identity section $\eta_G$ of the relative cotangent presheaf,
104	  \[
105	    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;:=\; \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k},
106	  \]
107	  is a finitely generated free $k$-module. We call $\mathfrak g^{\vee}$ the \emph{cotangent space of $G$ at the identity}. The \emph{Lie algebra of $G$} as a $k$-vector space is recovered as the $k$-linear dual $\mathfrak g := \mathfrak g^{\vee\vee} = \mathrm{Hom}_{k}(\mathfrak g^{\vee}, k)$ (the bracket structure is not used in the rigidity argument and is not packaged here).
108	
109	  \emph{Lean encoding note (iter-129 dualisation pinning).} The Lean declaration \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity} returns a \texttt{ModuleCat k} which is the \emph{un-dualised} cotangent $\mathfrak g^{\vee}$ — the direct output of the pullback-along-section construction described in the proof, with no internal \texttt{Module.Dual} hop. Downstream consumers that need the tangent $\mathfrak g$ take \texttt{Module.Dual k (cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G)} externally. This convention pins what was left open in the iter-128 encoding note: the rigidity argument is symmetric under dualisation (only the finite-rank-free claim is consumed), so the un-dualised form is preferred as it matches the body construction without an extra contravariant hop. The declaration embeds into Mathlib's \texttt{ModuleCat} infrastructure and supplies the \texttt{Module.Free k} / \texttt{Module.Finite k} typeclasses needed for the rank lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank} via the bridge lemma \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge}.

112	\begin{proof}
113	
114	\leanok
115	  The identity section $\eta_G$ is a canonical $k$-rational point of $G$ supplied by the $\GrpObj$ structure. The iter-131 Lean construction (Replacement (B), pure-term \texttt{noncomputable def}) realises $\mathfrak g^{\vee} = \eta_G^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ as a chart-base-changed Kähler module: by the forward Jacobian criterion \cref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} (already in the tree as \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth\_locally\_free\_omega} in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean}), there exists an affine chart $V \subseteq G$ around the image of the identity section $\eta_G(\mathrm{pt})$, with affine base chart $U \subseteq \Spec k$ and the structural ring map $\Gamma(\Spec k, U) \to \Gamma(G, V)$ exhibiting \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension} $n$ (and consequently $\Omega_{\Gamma(G,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}$ is a free $\Gamma(G, V)$-module of rank $n$ by \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} in \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}). The cotangent space at the identity is then defined as the base change to $k$ of this algebraic Kähler module along the ring map $\psi_V \colon \Gamma(G, V) \to k$ obtained by restricting the identity section $\eta_G$ to $V$ and composing with the canonical isomorphism $\Gamma(\Spec k, \cdot) \cong k$:
116	  \[
117	    \mathfrak g^{\vee} \;\;:=\;\; k \,\otimes_{\Gamma(G,\,V)}\, \Omega_{\Gamma(G,\,V) \,/\, \Gamma(\Spec k,\,U)}.
118	  \]
119	  This is a finitely generated free $k$-module of rank $n$ by base-change of a free module along $\psi_V$ (the rank is preserved; see \cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}).
120	
121	  \emph{Caveat on canonicity.} The construction depends on a chart choice $V$ extracted via a \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain from the existential statement of \texttt{smooth\_locally\_free\_omega}; it is therefore not canonically attached to $G$ in the strictest sense. (The iter-131 fix-up refactored what was previously a top-level \texttt{Classical.choice}-wrapper into a pure-term \texttt{let}-chain of \texttt{Classical.choose} / \texttt{Classical.choose\_spec} accessors, exposing the outer head symbol \texttt{(ModuleCat.extendScalars\,$\psi_V$.hom).obj\,(ModuleCat.of\,$\Gamma$(G, V)\,$\Omega$[$\Gamma$(G, V)\,/\,$\Gamma$(Spec k, U)])} to downstream consumers; see \S "Iter-131 \texttt{Classical.choose}-chain body shape" below for the Lean encoding details, and the companion \cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace_extendScalars_witness} for the structural-shape rewrite handle.) For the live consumer (rigidity over $k$, via \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}), the existence of a rank-$n$ free $k$-module is the only structural content needed, so canonicity is not load-bearing. A canonical (chart-independent) presentation via the stalk-side cotangent $\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}/\mathfrak m_{\eta_G}^{2}$ is available as a future bridge (\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangent_bridge} below, currently \texttt{\textbackslash notready}) at additional cost; the project defers that bridge until a non-rigidity consumer materialises that requires canonicity.
122	\end{proof}

The right-hand side $\pr_{2}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$ over $W$ gives $B \otimes_{B_{2}} \Omega_{B_{2}/k}$, where $B_{2}$ corresponds to the chart of $G$ obtained by restricting $\pr_{2}$. 1361	  \[
